$comment: The conformance matrix rendered on the gh-pages site. test/docs.test.ts enforces that every impl file exists and every proof names a real test in the referenced file — a claim cannot outlive its proof.

proofs.test: selects task-relevant, agent-audience, in-time units and ranks by score
not_for negative targeting skips a unit its publisher scoped out (spec §4)

rows.layer: Access is the auth axis — payment never substitutes for identity

proofs.test: fails closed on access:restricted without credentials, even when x402 is settleable
restricted units are never load-eligible when the agent holds no credentials
skips the predecessor on the overlap day when the successor is active
never selects a unit whose declared successor is itself selectable (spec §4.22)
gates a restricted unit when the agent cannot attest
allows the restricted unit once the agent presents a trusted provider
selects federation sub-manifests by env context and flags credential needs
fails closed: never fetches context-excluded or credential-gated refs
plans x402 payment and marks it unaffordable without the method
anonymous-paid the §4.11 way — access:public + x402 — is load-eligible without credentials
buys by score until the ceiling, skips what would blow it, keeps walking
resolves the rate-limit tier from agent credentials
projected spend of load-eligible units never exceeds the ceiling

rows.layer: Manifest signing (ed25519 over exact bytes, fail-closed)

proofs.test: fails closed on tampered content
verifies a JSON signature envelope with embedded key (the Cantara convention)
enforces requireSignature fail-closed on unsigned manifests
parses into the compact model
plans a typical task against itself
